D3 Chemistry L4 Analyze Reference

Binder Yellowing — Auto-oxidation Carbonyl Build-up

linseed-oil binders auto-oxidise — over years the unsaturated C=C bonds form C=O groups whose blue-absorption tints the film yellow

Drying oils (linseed, walnut, poppy) cure by auto-oxidative cross-linking — oxygen attacks the C=C bonds and forms peroxides, then carbonyls (C=O). Each carbonyl has a weak n→π* absorption around 350 nm. As they accumulate over years, the cumulative absorption tail reaches into the blue and the film yellows visibly. Storage in the dark slows the process; UV accelerates it.

Drag elapsed years (0–200). Carbonyl concentration follows first-order kinetics; the swatch on the right shows a once-white film yellowing; the absorption curve shows the blue-edge tail building up.
